info.title: ElectronSharp Tools (36.2.0.59099+e2e6df8b88e500c60b7a3ea97343e8da77ed2744)
info.description:
Project Home: https://github.com/ElectronSharp/ElectronSharp
Commands to start the Electron Application:
start                   Start your ASP.NET Core Application with Electron, without package it as a single exe. Faster for development.
Command to build the Electron Application:
build                   Build your Electron Application.
Command to init the Electron Application:
init                    Creates the needed ElectronSharp config for your Electron Application.
Command to add a custom npm packages to the Electron Application:
add                     The add command needs to be invoked via 'add hosthook'. This creates a special folder for your custom npm package installation.
Commands to see the current ElectronSharp version number:
version                 Displays the ElectronSharp.CLI version
To get help on individual commands execute:
electron-sharp help <command>

commands.name: start
commands.description:
Start your ASP.NET Core Application with Electron, without package it as a single exe. Faster for development.
Command to build the Electron Application:

arguments.name: --HELP =

RUNNING COMMAND: 'DOTNET PUBLISH -R LINUX-X64 -C "DEBUG" --OUTPUT "/TMP/INSPECTRA-HELP-ELECTRONSHARP.CLI-36.2.0.59099-553B59965CC14817BA803E7AE99E239F/OBJ/HOST/BIN" /P:PUBLISHREADYTORUN=FALSE /P:PUBLISHSINGLEFILE=FALSE --NO-SELF-CONTAINED /P:DISABLEDWARNINGS=TRUE' ON FOLDER '/TMP/INSPECTRA-HELP-ELECTRONSHARP.CLI-36.2.0.59099-553B59965CC14817BA803E7AE99E239F'

DOTNET PUBLISH -R LINUX-X64 -C "DEBUG" --OUTPUT "/TMP/INSPECTRA-HELP-ELECTRONSHARP.CLI-36.2.0.59099-553B59965CC14817BA803E7AE99E239F/OBJ/HOST/BIN" /P:PUBLISHREADYTORUN=FALSE /P:PUBLISHSINGLEFILE=FALSE --NO-SELF-CONTAINED /P:DISABLEDWARNINGS=TRUE
MSBUILD : ERROR MSB1003: SPECIFY A PROJECT OR SOLUTION FILE. THE CURRENT WORKING DIRECTORY DOES NOT CONTAIN A PROJECT OR SOLUTION FILE.
ERROR OCCURRED DURING DOTNET PUBLISH: 1

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build your Electron Application.
Command to init the Electron Application:

arguments.name: --HELP =
ERROR: MISSING 'TARGET' ARGUMENT.
NEEDED: '/TARGET' WITH PARAMS 'WIN/OSX/LINUX' TO BUILD FOR A TYPICAL APP OR USE 'CUSTOM' AND SPECIFY .NET CORE BUILD CONFIG & ELECTRON BUILD CONFIG
FOR CUSTOM TARGET, CHECK .NET CORE RID CATALOG AND ELECTRON BUILD TARGET/
E.G. '/TARGET WIN' OR '/TARGET CUSTOM "WIN7-X86;WIN"'
OPTIONAL: '/BINFOLDERNAME' THE OUTPUT FOLDER OF BINARY RESOURCE FILES. DEFAULT = BIN
OPTIONAL: '/DOTNET-CONFIGURATION' WITH THE DESIRED .NET CORE BUILD CONFIG E.G. RELEASE OR DEBUG. DEFAULT = RELEASE
OPTIONAL: '/ELECTRON-ARCH' TO SPECIFY THE RESULTING ELECTRON PROCESSOR ARCHITECTURE (E.G. IA86 FOR X86 BUILDS). BE AWARE TO USE THE '/TARGET CUSTOM' PARAM AS WELL!
OPTIONAL: '/ELECTRON-PARAMS' SPECIFY ANY OTHER VALID PARAMETER, WHICH WILL BE ROUTED TO THE ELECTRON-PACKAGER.
OPTIONAL: '/RELATIVE-PATH' TO SPECIFY OUTPUT A SUBDIRECTORY FOR OUTPUT.
OPTIONAL: '/ABSOLUTE-PATH TO SPECIFY AND ABSOLUTE PATH FOR OUTPUT.
OPTIONAL: '/PACKAGE-JSON' TO SPECIFY A CUSTOM PACKAGE.JSON FILE.
OPTIONAL: '/INSTALL-MODULES' TO FORCE NODE MODULE INSTALL. IMPLIED BY '/PACKAGE-JSON'
OPTIONAL: '/VERSION' TO SPECIFY THE VERSION THAT SHOULD BE APPLIED TO BOTH THE `DOTNET PUBLISH` AND `ELECTRON-BUILDER` COMMANDS. IMPLIED BY '/VERSION'
OPTIONAL: '/P:[PROPERTY]' OR '/PROPERTY:[PROPERTY]' TO PASS IN DOTNET PUBLISH PROPERTIES.
FULL EXAMPLE FOR A 32BIT DEBUG BUILD WITH ELECTRON PRUNE: BUILD /TARGET CUSTOM WIN7-X86;WIN32 /DOTNET-CONFIGURATION DEBUG /ELECTRON-ARCH IA32

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Creates the needed ElectronSharp config for your Electron Application.
Command to add a custom npm packages to the Electron Application:

arguments.name: --HELP =
ADDING OUR CONFIG FILE TO YOUR PROJECT...
SEARCH YOUR .CSPROJ/FSPROJ TO ADD THE NEEDED ELECTRON.MANIFEST.JSON...
FOUND YOUR :
arguments.description: - check for existing config or update it.
